title: com.nexus.bridge_pipeline — hourly launchd plist for the ω-bridge-18 full pipeline
summary: Add a committed launchd plist (config/launchd/com.nexus.bridge_pipeline.plist) that, once operator-loaded, runs `bash tool/bridge_health.sh -A -L --quiet` hourly at :15. Pairs with a discoverable .hexa install-help printer (tool/bridge_pipeline_install_help.hexa). ω-bridge-18 wired the full health → anomaly → alert pipeline behind one CLI invocation; ω-bridge-20 makes that invocation a scheduled system service rather than a manually-triggered one. Plist is committed but NOT loaded by this cycle — operator initiates via launchctl bootstrap when ready.

predecessor.omega_bridge_18: wired bridge_health.sh -A -L flags so a single invocation drives health → anomaly → alert end-to-end (commit 8025817e)
predecessor.gap: Nothing schedules `bash tool/bridge_health.sh -A -L --quiet`. Operators must run manually or maintain a custom cron entry. Operationalization missing.

design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: fires at unpredictable wall-clock times; overlaps with :00-aligned crons (atlas-meta-scan, omega-metrics) at unstable offsets
:00 is the cron stampede minute; competing IO with other plists

design_alternatives_considered.chosen_because: fires at :15 every hour, deterministic wall-clock cadence, off the :00 stampede; matches operator expectation for hourly ops

design_alternatives_considered.option: D — embed install instructions inline in plist comments only (option b in brief)
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: operator must know the plist path to read its comments; not discoverable via `hexa run tool/...`

design_alternatives_considered.option: E — sibling .hexa install-help printer (CHOSEN, in addition to plist comments)
design_alternatives_considered.chosen_because: self-discoverable via `hexa run tool/bridge_pipeline_install_help.hexa`; symmetric with other tool/*.hexa entries; raw-9 .hexa-only-suffix compliant

files_created_in_nexus.purpose: launchd LaunchAgent plist for the hourly bridge pipeline
files_created_in_nexus.env_minimization_rationale: bridge_health.sh resolves NEXUS_ROOT (defaults to $HOME/core/nexus) and HEXA_BIN (per-context auto-detect, ω-bridge-8) internally. Plist sets only PATH/HOME (POSIX baseline) + HEXA_LANG (resolver fallback, mirrors com.nexus.omega-metrics). Avoids env duplication.

files_created_in_nexus.purpose: discoverable install-help printer; runs as `hexa run tool/bridge_pipeline_install_help.hexa` and prints the launchctl ritual

naming_convention_research.chosen: com.nexus.bridge_pipeline (underscore preserved for symmetry with bridge_health.sh / bridge_anomaly.hexa filenames)

compliance.raw_9_hexa_only_suffix: .plist explicitly permitted (launchd-system-defined integration surface per cycle brief); .hexa printer is hexa-suffix
compliance.no_parallel_session_collision: no edits to cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own — verified via git status --short pre-write
compliance.explicit_path_commit: git add invoked with explicit paths only — config/launchd/com.nexus.bridge_pipeline.plist + tool/bridge_pipeline_install_help.hexa
compliance.no_launchctl_load: plist committed only; bootstrap/enable/kickstart deferred to operator (per cycle brief constraint)

log_dir_exists.result: exists (drwxr-xr-x, 33 entries) — no mkdir needed at runtime, but install ritual still includes `mkdir -p ~/.hx/log` for portability

install_help_hexa_runs.result: ec=0, prints schedule + install + status + uninstall + verify sections; all paths absolute

no_actual_launchctl_load.result: verified — only plutil + filesystem checks performed; launchctl bootstrap NOT invoked

operational_notes.headline_install_command: cp /Users/ghost/core/nexus/config/launchd/com.nexus.bridge_pipeline.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ && launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.nexus.bridge_pipeline.plist && launchctl enable gui/$UID/com.nexus.bridge_pipeline
operational_notes.back_compat: no impact on existing invocations; plist is additive and operator-opt-in

future_omega_bridge_21_candidates: rotate ~/.hx/log/bridge_pipeline.{log,err} via logrotate or in-script truncation when >N MB
extend install-help .hexa to optionally execute `cp + launchctl bootstrap` interactively (with --apply confirmation gate)
emit __OMEGA_BRIDGE_PIPELINE__ heartbeat sentinel from bridge_health.sh tail when ALERT_CHECK fires, so pipeline-driven alerts are distinguishable from manual-invocation alerts
cross-host plist generator that templates this for non-/Users/ghost installs
